content: I will update `js/app.js` using `multi_replace_file_content` to add `image` paths to all cafe menu items in the state, and update `renderCafeMenu` to draw the image elements instead of the icon visual placeholders.
thinking:
**Investigating Rendering Details**

I'm now focusing on `app.js`. Specifically, I noticed both `renderCafeMenu` and `renderGroceryStore` only display item names, not prices or descriptions. I'm checking for alternative card styling in the same file. I also checked `cafe`.


**Uncovering Interactive Elements**

I've been examining how menu items become actionable. I initially thought the listings in `cafe.html` or `grocery.html` were the primary interaction points. Instead, I found they are purely informational. Then I looked for an `onclick` on the cafe cards, and found nothing. However, digging into `app.js`, I discovered `handleCafeAddClick`. Lines 520-540 contain this function. Now I'm tracing its execution; it might be called on a click, even without the expected button or `onclick` event listener!

args.Description: "Added image properties to state.cafeMenu and updated renderCafeMenu in js/app.js to display real images."
args.Instruction: "Add image paths to all cafeMenu items in state, and display img elements in renderCafeMenu cards."
